one_line_thesis: Acadian Timber owns ~1.1M acres in Maine and New Brunswick and manages ~1.3M acres of Canadian Crown Lands — lean, income-oriented timberland in the land sleeve pending NAV overlay.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Acadian Timber is a lean, acreage-heavy forest operator: New Brunswick freehold and Crown management provide the cash engine; Maine freehold is transitioning to internal harvesting after contractor capacity issues; carbon credits and real estate remain optional upside layers. FY2025 owner cash troughed at CAD 0.37 per share with no carbon sales, while the quarterly CAD 0.29 dividend continued, supported by Macer’s full DRIP participation and a solid balance sheet (CAD 17.4 million net liquidity, CAD 110 million long-term debt). At CAD 17.35, proof-first component economic value sums to ~CAD...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Base zero probability until next verified Katahdin voluntary credit issuance; high case uses one repeat of the 2024 CAD 24.6M sale precedent.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Project monitoring and verification costs are embedded in operating segments; no incremental owner-funded capital reserved in base case.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Base assigns zero until next verified issuance; real-estate fees remain small.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

options.evidence: Primary bridge from ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf: FY2025 FCF $0.37/sh, book $19.67/sh, net liquidity $17.4M, debt $110.009M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: No disclosed carbon issuance schedule in FY2025 filings; real-estate HBU reviews are episodic.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Base zero probability until next verified Katahdin voluntary credit issuance; high case uses one repeat of the 2024 CAD 24.6M sale precedent.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Project monitoring and verification costs are embedded in operating segments; no incremental owner-funded capital reserved in base case.

options.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Base assigns zero until next verified issuance; real-estate fees remain small.
options.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 annual report: book value $19.67/sh; timberlands at historical cost less depletion; economic value uses owner cash and component NAV, not GAAP book alone.

economic_claim.description: One ADN.TO common share claim on harvest and Crown fee cash flows, owned timberland, carbon/real-estate options, net financial position, less payout and cycle reserve.
economic_claim.unit_source: 18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026 (ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-2026-Q1-Interim-Report.pdf)
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Harvest owner cash, owned timberland IFRS NAV, carbon/real-estate options, net liquidity and debt, and payout reserve are separate overlap keys; Crown fee stream stays in producing operations only.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Consolidated harvest and Crown management fee stream

valuation_proof.adjustment: Excludes lumpy 2024 carbon credit sales from base owner-cash rate.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 interim report: Maine internal logging ramp continues; NB Adj EBITDA margin 30% vs Maine 9%; quarterly dividend C$0.29 maintained; 18,619,712 shares outstanding
FY2025: declared dividends C$1.16/sh while Free Cash Flow troughed at C$0.37/sh with zero carbon credit sales after C$24.6M sale in 2024
Established internal logging operations in Maine to address contractor capacity and trucking constraints
Acquired Maine harvesting and trucking assets for C$6.9M cash to support internal harvest transition
Sold 752,100 voluntary carbon credits for C$24.6M (Anew Katahdin Forestry Project, Maine easement); no repeat in FY2025
Renewed Sustainable Forestry Initiative certification for New Brunswick and Maine operations with no non-conformances
Malcolm Cockwell serving as interim CEO per Q1 2026 shareholder letter; permanent appointment pending human review
Conversion from income trust to corporation; quarterly dividend framework continues on TSX
IPO and TSX listing as Acadian Timber income fund; Macer Forest Holdings remains controlling shareholder (~52% with 100% DRIP)

industry.structure: Regional Northeast softwood and hardwood log markets with ~85 mill customers; Acadian is a major freehold owner in New Brunswick and Maine plus fee manager on ~1.33M Crown licensed acres. Pricing follows mill demand and fuel-adjustment mechanics; no national pricing power.
industry.share_shift: Acadian is gaining internal harvest share in Maine after contractor shortages; New Brunswick volumes shift between Crown services and freehold harvest depending on contractor availability and customer mix.
industry.trend: Weak lumber end-use markets and tariff-related hardwood pulp uncertainty persist in 2025–2026; voluntary carbon credits and real-estate or recreation leases remain episodic upside layers on a timber income base.

top_claims.claim: Acadian Timber is a lean, acreage-heavy forest operator: New Brunswick freehold and Crown management provide the cash engine; Maine freehold is transitioning to internal harvesting after contractor capacity issues; carbon credits and real estate remain optional upside layers. FY2025 owner cash troughed at CAD 0.37 per
*Primary risk: Dividend exceeds sustainable Free Cash Flow after carbon sales normalize, forcing DRIP dilution, debt use, or a dividend reset that re-rates the yield-focused holder base.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Dividend exceeds sustainable Free Cash Flow after carbon sales normalize, forcing DRIP dilution, debt use, or a dividend reset that re-rates the yield-focused holder base.
